creator: freaky@aagh.net
text:
* Anton Berezin (tobez@tobez.org) wrote:

> On Fri, Nov 23, 2001 at 09:09:34PM +0000, Thomas Hurst wrote:
>
> >     Additionally, the build of world seems to ignore $LOCALBASE in
> >     setting the compiled-in value of @INC in perl, so if the modules
> >     *were* installed in the correct location they wouldn't actually
> >     work.  Sigh :)
>
> I do not think it is a good idea to make perl world build aware of
> port-specific LOCALBASE variable.

Port-specific?  LOCALBASE isn't port specific (which suggests specific
to a single port), it's global for the entire ports tree.

> I guess this can be discussed but I really think that for this one you
> might be better off specifying PERL5LIB in your /etc/login.conf using
> setenv capability.

This is ignored when perl's in taint mode or suid, relying on the
compiled-in @INC; if I have ports set to install to /usr/pkg (this being
the entire reason I noticed this issue), Perl won't use them, despite
that being a trusted location.

-- 
Thomas 'Freaky' Hurst  -  freaky@aagh.net  -  http://www.aagh.net/
